title: Why the First Five Years Are Your Child's Biliteracy Superpower
titleEs: Por Qué los Primeros Cinco Años Son el Superpoder de Biliteracidad de Tu Hijo
excerpt: Someone probably said it to you at a family dinner. 'Won't two languages confuse them?' It's said with the best intentions. And it's one of the most persistent myths in parenting.
excerptEs: Probablemente alguien te lo dijo en una cena familiar. El bilingüismo no confunde a los niños — los fortalece.
pullQuote: The first five years don't just shape language — they shape the brain that will learn every language that follows.
body:
Someone probably said it to you at a family dinner, or maybe at a playgroup. "Won't two languages confuse them?" It's said with the best intentions. It sounds like caution. And it's one of the most persistent myths in parenting.

The truth is the opposite. The first five years of your child's life are the single greatest window for language learning that will ever exist.

## What the Brain Is Actually Doing Before Age Five

Young children are, neurologically speaking, extraordinary. Before five, the brain is primed for phonemic awareness — the ability to hear and distinguish the distinct sounds of language. In a bilingual home, your child is building two complete sound systems at the same time.

[PULLQUOTE]

## The Mixing Is Normal. The Mixing Is Good.

One of the things that worries parents most is when their child mixes languages mid-sentence. This is called codeswitching, and it is a sign of a developing bilingual brain doing exactly what it should.

## Four Things That Actually Make a Difference

Read in both languages, every single day. Don't stress about keeping languages perfectly separate. Make the second language feel alive in the world. Find community in the language.

## You Don't Have to Wait for School

The home is the original classroom. Do it in two languages. Start today. The window is open — and it is beautiful while it lasts.
bodyEs:
La verdad es lo opuesto. Los primeros cinco años de vida de tu hijo son la ventana más grande para el aprendizaje de idiomas que jamás existirá.

[PULLQUOTE]

La mezcla de idiomas es normal y buena. Se llama cambio de código, y es una señal de que el cerebro bilingüe en desarrollo está haciendo exactamente lo que debe.
